title: 'Uniclear' water-based brain clearing for light sheet imaging of electrode tracks
author: Susu Chen
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zndf5a6

steps.title: Perfusion and Brain Fixation
steps.instruction: Perfuse mouse with cold 4% PFA. Post fix brain for at least 24:00:00 at room temperature (RT). Wash brain in PBS to remove fixative. Fixed brain samples can be kept in PBS at 4 °C before further processing.

steps.title: Brain Delipidation
steps.instruction: Delipidate brain with SBiP buffer: 20 mL per adult mouse brain, shaking (70 RPM) at 37 °C . On the first day: change buffer at 03:00:00 , 06:00:00 , 12:00:00 and leave over night. Starting on the second day: change SBiP buffer every 24:00:00 for consecutive 14 days , shaking (70 RPM) at 37 °C .

steps.title: Post-Delipidation Wash
steps.instruction: After delipidation, wash brain in PBS for at least 12:00:00 before further processing. Sample can be stored in PBS at RT before refractive index matching.

steps.title: Refractive Index Matching
steps.instruction: Refractive index matching: first day in 20 mL 50% PBS + 50% opti-prep mixed solution, rotating at RT for 24:00:00 . On second day transfer the brain to 40 mL 100% opti-prep solution, rotating at RT for 24:00:00 . Index-matched brain should appear transparent.

steps.title: Light-Sheet Imaging Setup
steps.instruction: For light-sheet imaging (e.g. Zeiss Z1) mount brain vertically. Example of a mounted brain (fluorescent yellow color is DNA dye YO-PRO, which is optional). For electrode localization we use autofluorescence. To image tissue autofluorescence use 488 nm laser. To image CM-Dil tracks use 561 nm laser. Brain can be imaged with a voxel size of 1.22 µm x 1.22 µm x 8 µm on Z1 with a 5x objective.
